title: TermsAndConditionsLinterReport
description: JSON Schema for the linter output produced by Zenix Sign's TocLinterService when validating a TermsAndConditionsVersion content. Persisted in TermsAndConditionsVersion.linterReport (jsonb).

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of this schema. Allows future migrations.

ranAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the linter executed against the content.

ranByStaffId.description: Staff ID that triggered the lint (null if automatic on save).

language.description: Language of the linted content. Rules are language-aware (sectionHeading regex differs).

contentSha256.description: SHA-256 of the linted content (so we can detect content drift between lint and activation).

findings.description: Array of all issues detected. Empty array if content passed cleanly.

summary.description: Aggregated counts by severity for quick UI badges.

errors.description: Count of findings with severity='error'. Activation of the ToC version is blocked while >0.

warnings.description: Count of findings with severity='warning'. Activation allowed with explicit override + reason.

info.description: Count of findings with severity='info'. Suggestions, never blocking.

rulesetVersion.description: Version of the rule pack that produced this report (allows tracking which ruleset version was used). Format: semver.

ruleId.description: Unique identifier of the rule that fired. Stable across versions of the same rule (SemVer of rule = $defs/rulesetVersion).

severity.description: error = bloqueante para activación de la versión; warning = activable con override + reason; info = sugerencia.

message.description: Human-readable message in the language of the content. Localized.

messageEn.description: Optional English translation for internal support reviews.

location.description: Where in the document the issue was found. Null if global (e.g., missing section).

line.description: 1-based line number in the markdown content.

column.description: 1-based column. Null if rule applies to the whole line.

snippet.description: Excerpt of the offending text (truncated to 200 chars) for the editor highlight.

suggestion.description: Optional auto-fix suggestion. Null if no actionable replacement available.

kind.description: replace = swap snippet for newText; insert = add newText at location; delete = remove snippet; manual = no auto-fix, only described.

description.description: Human description of the suggested fix.

newText.description: The replacement/insertion text. Null when kind='delete' or kind='manual'.

reference.description: External authoritative reference backing the rule (regulatory citation).

citation.description: Short canonical citation. Example: 'PROFECO LFPC Art. 90' or 'Visa CRR 13.7'.

url.description: Direct URL to the official source if publicly available.

overridable.description: Si true, el supervisor puede activar la versión a pesar del finding con override + reason. Si false (solo aplicable a severity='error'), bloquea activación obligatoriamente.

overrideHistory.description: Historial de overrides previos al guardar (auditoría). Vacío en primera ejecución.

findings.message: La ventana de aviso para cambios de fecha es de 16 días hábiles. Plazos mayores a 3 días hábiles pueden considerarse cláusula abusiva según PROFECO Art. 90.
findings.messageEn: Date change notice window is 16 business days. Windows greater than 3 business days may be deemed abusive under PROFECO Art. 90.

location.snippet: Requests for date modifications must be submitted with a minimum of 16 business days' notice prior to the original check-in date.

suggestion.description: Reducir la ventana a 72 horas o 3 días hábiles, alineado con estándares de industria boutique.
suggestion.newText: Requests for date modifications must be submitted with a minimum of 72 hours' notice prior to the original check-in date.

findings.message: Cargo de USD 150 por toalla perdida puede considerarse desproporcionado. Estándar de industria boutique es USD 25-50.
findings.messageEn: USD 150 charge per lost towel may be deemed disproportionate. Industry standard for boutique hotels is USD 25-50.

location.snippet: Lost or removed towels will be charged USD 150.

suggestion.description: Ajustar a tarifa proporcional al costo real de reposición.
suggestion.newText: Lost or removed towels will be charged USD 40.

reference.citation: HFTP Hospitality Financial Management Handbook 2023 — Section 4.7 Ancillary Charges
